findings.title: Quote API lacks ready-path contract coverage
findings.why_it_matters: The quote endpoint is the widget's executable API boundary. Current tests can pass while the ready path stops emitting usable approval, swap, route hash, deadline, slippage, or bigint-serialized quote fields, because the tested cases never force a successful live quote response.
findings.suggested_fix: Introduce an injectable handler/helper for readiness and quote adapter creation, then add a POST test with fake ready readiness and quote evidence that asserts the ready JSON contract, approval/swap shape, routeHash, expiresAt, and slippage/deadline handling.
evidence: route.ts:215-237 selects the live async quote path only when readiness is ready, then route.ts:251-275 serializes the executable API contract including routeHash, approval, swap, and route.
route.test.ts:15-75 only covers router override rejection, overlong amount rejection, and missing-RPC quote_adapter_failure. It never exercises a 200 ready response.
The endpoint constructs createPublicClient/createLiveLiquidityQuoteAdapter internally, so the current test surface has no seam for deterministic ready-path coverage without live RPC.

findings.title: Quote hook fetch lifecycle is untested
findings.why_it_matters: This hook decides when the widget calls the API, when it stays local, how stale requests are aborted, and how API failures become non-executable quote_adapter_failure quotes. A regression here can leave the widget showing stale executable data or skipping the safe failure state even though the deserializer tests still pass.
findings.suggested_fix: Add hook-level tests with a mocked fetch and controllable readiness/recipient inputs for amount-null reset, not-ready or missing-recipient local quotes, ready POST body construction, non-OK error conversion, and stale/aborted response suppression.
evidence: useFameSwapQuote.ts:425-512 has branches for amount/key reset, local blocked quotes, live fetch, non-OK response handling, cancellation, AbortError, and generic fetch failures.
useFameSwapQuote.test.ts:38-132 imports and tests deserializeFameSwapQuoteResponse only. It does not render or invoke useFameSwapQuote and does not mock fetch.
No changed test file references fetch(), AbortError, refresh(), or the hook result fields isLoading/error/quoteKey.

findings.title: Widget actions lack behavioral tests
findings.why_it_matters: The component wires amount validation, balance checks, readiness, wallet chain switching, approvals, swaps, and reset into a single primary CTA. Pure state tests do not prove the rendered widget disables or dispatches the correct action at the point where users can spend funds.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a component harness or extract a tested CTA decision helper covering invalid/excess amounts, wrong-chain switch, approval_needed submitApproval, ready submitSwap, confirmed reset, and advanced slippage/deadline propagation into the quote hook.
evidence: FameSwapWidget.tsx:231-240 computes primaryDisabled from state, readiness, amountBlocked, transaction capabilities, and switching/submitting state.
FameSwapWidget.tsx:248-269 handles the primary action branches for disconnected/no-op, chain switch, approval, swap, and confirmed reset.
FameSwapWidget.test.ts:12-39 only exercises quoteSummary. state.test.ts covers the pure fameSwapWidgetState mapper but not rendered wiring, hook inputs, button disabled state, or action dispatch.

findings.title: V4 overflow guard lacks regression
findings.why_it_matters: The Uniswap V4 adapter has an explicit fail-closed guard before passing exactAmount into a uint128 quoter field. Without a regression test, a later refactor could remove or move the guard and only fail against live RPC with an encoding or revert error instead of a safe adapter rejection.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a liveAdapters test using a Uniswap V4 edge with amountIn = 1n << 128n, assert status failed/reason adapter_failure/message mentions uint128, and assert the quoter read is not called.
evidence: liveAdapters.ts:713-718 returns adapter_failure when request.amountIn exceeds MAX_UINT128.
liveAdapters.test.ts:361-412 covers the normal V4 quote request and asserts exactAmount: 12_345n, but no test uses an amount above the uint128 boundary.
Repository search for MAX_UINT128, uint128, and 'exceeds Uniswap V4' finds only the implementation, not a regression assertion.

findings.title: Route-lab simulation path is untested
findings.why_it_matters: The route lab is used as validation evidence, but the --simulate path contains separate approval-bundled simulation, native swap simulation, protected-minimum recalculation, and failure redaction logic. Bugs there can report false validation or leak provider diagnostics while the current recorded/deterministic tests stay green.
findings.suggested_fix: Expose or inject the simulation helper/client seam and add fake-client tests for missing account, non-ready skip, approval-then-swap success, native swap success, protected-minimum rerun, and failed simulation redaction.
evidence: fame-swap-route-lab.ts:207-333 implements simulateQuote with not-ready, no-client, no-account, approve-then-swap simulateCalls, direct simulateContract, protected minimum, and catch/redaction branches.
fame-swap-route-lab.ts:448-545 is the live route-lab path that calls simulateQuote when options.simulate is true.
fame-swap-route-lab.test.ts:12-104 covers recorded replay, deterministic replay, and markdown redaction only. It does not call runLiveRouteLab or exercise any simulation branch.

findings.title: Fork harness has no unit-test seams
findings.why_it_matters: The fork smoke harness now owns case selection, artifact hash validation, RPC proxying, local router readiness, token balance seeding, and protected-route execution evidence. With no unit-testable helpers, regressions in env parsing, redaction, hash checks, or selected cases are only caught by a full Anvil/RPC run, which is expensive and often skipped.
findings.suggested_fix: Move pure helper logic into a side-effect-free module and add node:test coverage for FAME_SWAP_FORK_CASES default/all/empty/unknown handling, displaySafeProcessOutput redaction, artifact hash mismatch reporting with temp fixtures, and wrapper env generation/restoration behavior.
evidence: fame-swap-fork-smoke.ts:204-228 parses FAME_SWAP_FORK_CASES and throws for empty or unknown ids, but the function is private and the script executes main at import time.
fame-swap-fork-smoke.ts:437-445 redacts process output, and fame-swap-fork-smoke.ts:546-585 verifies all artifact hashes, but there is no changed fame-swap-fork-smoke.test.ts or helper test.

package.json adds fame-swap:fork, fame-swap:fork-smoke, and fame-swap:local-dev scripts, while scripts/fame-swap-fork.ts and scripts/fame-swap-local-dev.ts also have untested env write/restore and child-process line parsing logic.

residual_risks: I did not execute the test suite; findings are based on tracked diff inspection only.
Generated wagmi output was not reviewed for dedicated test coverage because it appears code-generated and outside the requested focus.
testing_gaps: No deterministic ready-path contract test for POST /api/fame/swap/quote.
No hook-level tests for useFameSwapQuote fetch/local/abort/error behavior.
No rendered FameSwapWidget tests for CTA gating and wallet action dispatch.
No regression test for Uniswap V4 uint128 overflow fail-closed behavior.
No unit-testable coverage for fork smoke/fork/local-dev harness helper behavior.
